reasoning: The FRED trend analysis estimates a 0.33 probability of the yield exceeding the 7.34 threshold in the short term, due to a recent downward trend (from a peak of 7.74 in late April to 7.34 in early June). Overall, the yield is currently above its trailing one-year median of approximately 7.10, indicating it is likely to continue mean-reverting downward over the coming months as credit spreads potentially narrow or interest rates eventually soften. For the brief 7-day forecast (June 15), the established downward momentum strongly favors a decrease (P=0.35). As the forecast horizon extends to July, September, and December, uncertainty naturally increases, pulling the probability of an increase closer to the base rate of 0.50. However, the probability remains below 0.50 for all dates because the current yield is comparatively elevated relative to 2024 averages.
